justification: All 7 business rules identified with correct invariant classification. BR-7 (refund window) correctly marked as invariant: false with clear reasoning. Cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction explicitly captured in BR-1. Discount/promotion mutual exclusivity covered in BR-4. Multiple ambiguities surfaced (reservation boundary precision, delivery definition implicit in refund window discussion, discount conflict resolution priority, promotion timing during in-flight checkout). 9 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the 4 minimum. Confidence level stated at 82% with detailed assumptions.

observations.text: All 7 rules identified with correct invariant/non-invariant classification
BR-7 refund window correctly classified as invariant: false with excellent justification distinguishing policy from structural invariants
Cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction explicitly and clearly captured in BR-1
9 acceptance criteria in proper Given/When/Then format, far exceeding the 4 minimum
Rich edge case analysis covering race conditions, boundary precision, mid-checkout promotion changes, and chargebacks

Multiple ambiguities surfaced: reservation boundary precision (inclusive/exclusive 15 min), discount conflict resolution priority, promotion timing during in-flight checkout, partial checkout failure semantics
Comprehensive ubiquitous language glossary establishing shared terminology
Business impact and stakeholder analysis adds depth beyond minimum requirements

Bounded contexts could be more sharply separated — 'Order & Fulfillment Context' is presented as a single large context rather than clearly delineating 'cart and checkout lifecycle' vs 'inventory management' as distinct bounded contexts

The specific ambiguity about whether the 15-minute timer starts at checkout page load or first item addition was not called out in exactly those terms, though reservation boundary precision was flagged

agent_output_excerpt:
# Domain Analysis — E-Commerce Order & Inventory Rules

---

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context

This encodes the core purchasing lifecycle of an e-commerce platform: from cart management through checkout, payment, and post-sale refund. The rules govern **inventory reservation**, **promotional pricing**, **order immutability**, and **refund eligibility** — all critical to revenue integrity and customer trust.

### Ubiquitous Language

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Cart** | A transien
